commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 2 Chronicles 32:2 And when Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come, and that he was purposed to fight against Jerusalem, Praise: literally, "confess. "(Haydock) There is a confession of God's perfections, as well as of sin. (Worthington) Gates, within their respective courts. (Calmet) The Turks style the palace of their emperor, "the sublime porte. "(Haydock) Camp, or temple. Septuagint, "in the gates, in the courts of the house of the Lord. "(Menochius) Grabe has not in the gates. (Haydock) We know that they did not sing there, but in the courts, (Calmet) or halls.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
